_comment: Declarative least-privilege Cloudflare token suite. Each entry mints one scoped token (via manage-tokens.mjs) stored in GCP Secret Manager under `secret`. `policies` map directly to Cloudflare token policies: scope 'account' → the whole account; scope 'zones' → all zones (com.cloudflare.api.account.zone.*). permission_groups are matched by exact name against /user/tokens/permission_groups — a name that doesn't resolve FAILS the run (never silently under-scopes). Run `--plan` to list the live group names if any don't resolve.

tokens.name: workers-deploy
tokens.purpose: Generic wrangler Worker deploys: script + routes + Durable Objects, with READ on every binding type for deploy-time validation. No resource-create power (that is cf-token-infra). DOs need no group — they ride on Workers Scripts Write.

tokens.name: cache-purge
tokens.purpose: Edge cache purge (the dedicated purge token the policy requires — deploy tokens lack this)

tokens.name: dns
tokens.purpose: DNS records + custom-domain reconciliation (cf-domain-reconcile, preview-domain lifecycle, qa-tools-ui). DNS Write is in NO deploy token by design.

tokens.name: infra
tokens.purpose: Resource PROVISIONING only — create/destroy D1, KV, R2, Queues, Hyperdrive, Vectorize. Used by provision-* / add-new-app and create-if-absent steps. Isolated from routine deploys so a leaked deploy token cannot create or wipe infrastructure.

tokens.name: supervisor-deploy
tokens.purpose: factory-supervisor Worker deploy — includes D1 + Vectorize for RFC-007 semantic memory. Once created, update GitHub secret CF_API_TOKEN to this value. See issue #1750.
